message:
docs(readme): fix grossly stale FP-P2D packet range/count (br-78ttq / fd90.149)

fd90.149: line 1449 claimed:
  '... plus 30+ DataFrame-level packets (FP-P2D-014 through FP-P2D-055)
   covering merge, concat axis options, head/tail, loc/iloc, sort,
   constructor variants, and more.'

But the actual P2D coverage in crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/
is much larger:
  ls fixtures/packets/ | grep -oE 'fp_p2d_[0-9]+' | sort -u | wc -l
  → 419 distinct P2D packet IDs

  range: fp_p2d_014 through fp_p2d_433

The README understates the count by ~14× (30+ → 419+) and the range
ceiling by ~8× (055 → 433). Likely from an early-Phase-2D snapshot
that was never refreshed as new packets landed.

Fix: bump '30+' → '400+' and '055' → '433+' to reflect actual
coverage. The operation-family list (merge / concat / head/tail / etc)
stays — it's qualitative coverage, not a count claim.

Pure README change. No code impact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-76mw","title":"fd90.40: SqlInspector::reflect_table for bundled table metadata","description":"Add SqlReflectedTable struct (table_name, columns, primary_key_columns, indexes, foreign_keys, unique_constraints, comment) and SqlInspector::reflect_table(table, schema) -> Result<Option<SqlReflectedTable>, IoError> that bundles all 5 introspection calls into one fetch. Mirrors SQLAlchemy.MetaData.reflect_table(). Returns Ok(None) when the table doesn't exist (i.e. table_schema returns None). Pure-delegation slice — calls table_schema, primary_key_columns, list_indexes, list_foreign_keys, list_unique_constraints, table_comment in sequence. Tests cover unknown table -> None, real SQLite table populating columns + PK + index, multi-schema PG-like routing, comment propagated through bundle.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:10:09.123798115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:13:14.391172165Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:13:14.388687912Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd90","review","sql"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-78ttq","title":"fd90.149: README claims 30+ FP-P2D packets through 055; actual is 419+ packets through 433","description":"fd90.74-148 README audit. Conformance Testing in Depth section at line 1449 claims:

  '... plus 30+ DataFrame-level packets (FP-P2D-014 through FP-P2D-055) covering merge, concat axis options, head/tail, loc/iloc, sort, constructor variants, and more.'

But the actual fp-conformance fixture packet count for P2D is much higher:
  ls crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/ | grep -oE 'fp_p2d_[0-9]+' | sort -u | wc -l
  → 419 distinct P2D packet IDs

And the range:
  earliest: fp_p2d_014
  latest:   fp_p2d_433 (verified with sort -u | tail)

So:
- '30+ DataFrame-level packets' → actually 419+
- 'FP-P2D-014 through FP-P2D-055' → actually 014 through 433

The README's range claim caps at 055 — off by a factor of ~10× on count and ~7-8× on range.

Fix: update both the count ('30+' → '400+') and the range ('014 through 055' → '014 through 433+') to reflect actual coverage. The list of operations covered ('merge, concat axis options, head/tail, loc/iloc, sort, constructor variants, and more') is fine.

Pure README change. No code impact.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:14:13.323083764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:15:41.153093405Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:15:41.149639554Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-79wko","title":"fd90.147: frankenpandas Cargo.toml description still says '7 IO formats' — README + badge say 8 (fd90.89)","description":"fd90.74-146 audit. crates/frankenpandas/Cargo.toml package description (line 3) says:
  description = 'Clean-room Rust reimplementation of the pandas API — DataFrame, Series, GroupBy, Rolling, Resample, MultiIndex, 7 IO formats. Zero unsafe.'

But fd90.89 already updated the README from '7 IO formats' to '8 IO formats' (CSV/JSON/JSONL/Parquet/Excel/Feather/IPC/SQL) — the IPC stream format makes 8. The Cargo.toml description is stale and shows up on crates.io and 'cargo search', misleading users.

Fix: change '7 IO formats' to '8 IO formats' in the Cargo.toml description. Pure metadata change. No code impact.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:03:17.940297405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:05:07.128257319Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:05:07.125626658Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-7a49","title":"fd90.48: bulk-cfg-gate tests using make_sql_test_conn","description":"Continuation of fd90.46. Tests using make_sql_test_conn fail to compile under cargo test --no-default-features because the helper references rusqlite::Connection unconditionally. Bulk-add cfg sql-sqlite to make_sql_test_conn AND every test that calls it. Pre-existing tests that do not use the helper (CSV, JSON, Excel) are unaffected. Approach: python script that finds test functions whose body calls make_sql_test_conn and inserts the cfg attribute before test. ~67 such tests; mechanical change. Verify both feature profiles pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:10:34.954889801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T23:15:48.585893784Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T23:15:48.583398207Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","fd90","review","sql","test"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-7cfm","title":"[HIGH] docs: 0 of 1530 pub fns document '# Panics:' + no cargo doc gate in CI — public API contract underspecified","description":"Rustdoc + panic-contract audit across the public API surface.

**Numbers (fresh grep across crates/*/src/lib.rs):**
- Total `pub fn` definitions: 1530
- pub fns documenting `# Panics:`: 0
- cargo-doc step in ci.yml: absent
- `#![warn(missing_docs)]` or `#![deny(missing_docs)]` in any crate root: absent
- RUSTDOCFLAGS in ci.yml: unset (so `-D warnings` for rustdoc isn't applied)

**Observation.** fp-frame alone has 3836 `unwrap()` / `expect()` / `panic!` / `unreachable!` sites (fp-io: 546; fp-expr: 131). The overwhelming majority are in `#[test]` bodies (grep-verified on a sample) which is fine. BUT with zero `# Panics:` rustdoc comments across 1530 pub fns, a user cannot mechanically determine:
- Which pub fns assume non-empty Series and panic on empty?
- Which pub fns panic on mixed-dtype input vs returning Err?
- Which pub fns assume sorted indices?

The library's own conformance harness uses `.expect("value")` patterns (see fp-io/src/lib.rs:3663+), which is correct for test code but sets no pattern for user-facing docs.

**Consequence.** When a downstream Python binding (planned per br-diic) or a Rust library user hits an undocumented panic, they have no spec to reference. The `# Panics:` contract is pandas-aligned too: pandas' equivalent is "raises ValueError" in its rustdoc-analog (docstrings), and users rely on it.

**Severity.** HIGH — public API contract is underspecified. Downstream integrations (PyO3 wrapper when it ships, other Rust libs) have to read source to learn what panics vs errors.

**Fix shape:**
1. Add `#![warn(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]`, `#![warn(rustdoc::missing_doc_code_examples)]`, and `#![warn(missing_docs)]` to each `crates/*/src/lib.rs`. Start as warn, promote to deny once the doc backlog clears.
2. Add ci.yml step: `RUSTDOCFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo doc --workspace --no-deps --all-features`. Fails on any rustdoc warning.
3. Audit pass: for each pub fn that can panic (not returning `Result`), add a `# Panics:` section. Prioritize fp-frame public API — the largest public surface.
4. Optional: use `#[cfg_attr(debug_assertions, track_caller)]` on panicking pub fns so panic locations are clear in user tracebacks.

Blast radius: crate root attribute flip in 12 crates, ci.yml (+1 doc step), audit pass (~500-800 lines of doc additions; parallelizable across agents, one pub fn at a time).

First-wave priority order: fp-frame > fp-io > fp-expr > fp-groupby > fp-join (most-used downstream).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:28:20.838989269Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:54:04.092826630Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T22:54:04.090813177Z","close_reason":"Added workspace rustdoc CI gate and first-wave public # Panics docs in fp-frame","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api-contract","docs","review","rustdoc"]}

@@ -1446,7 +1446,7 @@ The conformance system is a differential testing framework that verifies Franken

 ```
 
-**Packet families** cover: series alignment (FP-P2C-001–003), join semantics (FP-P2C-004), groupby aggregates (FP-P2C-005, 011), concat (FP-P2C-006), null/NaN ops (FP-P2C-007), CSV round-trip (FP-P2C-008), dtype invariants (FP-P2C-009), filter/selection (FP-P2C-010), plus 30+ DataFrame-level packets (FP-P2D-014 through FP-P2D-055) covering merge, concat axis options, head/tail, loc/iloc, sort, constructor variants, and more.
+**Packet families** cover: series alignment (FP-P2C-001–003), join semantics (FP-P2C-004), groupby aggregates (FP-P2C-005, 011), concat (FP-P2C-006), null/NaN ops (FP-P2C-007), CSV round-trip (FP-P2C-008), dtype invariants (FP-P2C-009), filter/selection (FP-P2C-010), plus 400+ DataFrame-level packets (FP-P2D-014 through FP-P2D-433+) covering merge, concat axis options, head/tail, loc/iloc, sort, constructor variants, and more.
 
 Every parity report gets a **RaptorQ repair-symbol sidecar** for bit-rot detection. The drift history ledger (`artifacts/phase2c/drift_history.jsonl`) tracks parity trends over time.
